feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /int/ International: Where are you sitting?

entry.title: /b/ Random: Question for old timers
entry.content: What is it like getting old? I'm in my mid 20s and I wonder what it is going to be like being in my 30s and 40s. Do you lose interest in stuff you used to like?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: If the RTX 6080 doesn't beat the RTX 5090 then the 60 series will be yet another…
entry.content: If the RTX 6080 doesn't beat the RTX 5090 then the 60 series will be yet another complete disappointment on top of RTX 40 and 50. GPU tech as we know it might be actually DEAD if RTX 60 doesn't deliver. Maybe AMD can save the next generation by having a card that beats the 4090 at least?
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: I basedfaced when I saw all the scenes & characters from Type Help finally pictu…
entry.content: I basedfaced when I saw all the scenes & characters from Type Help finally pictured from my imagination after a couple repeat playthroughs of the original. The voice-acting is incredible, and the sound design really adds to the atmosphere. A big improvement over the silent terminal interface. It seems like the deduction is more rail-roaded compared to the original, though.
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: It's time to create a new world. We just need 3 strikes. I'm waiting for the fir…
entry.content: It's time to create a new world. We just need 3 strikes. I'm waiting for the first strike No pain, no sorrow that world will have. All you will disappear, my friends. From Earth, it will last nothing. I'm the only one who can create and destroy. I hope that I fight all the five knights of the apocalypse and kill Lucifer and defeat God, so that I'm conceded the power of creation. We're/I'm going to live in a happier world without pain and sorrow. I promise.
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.content: did you know they got their names from the case that printer / typographers used to hold the metal letter dies. And I just let some weird Emo guy talk me into doing something I'm ashamed of (and I don't want to say exactly what I did because I'm embarrassed, but it involved him defecating into my mouth)
entry.dc:creator: /b/ Random

entry.title: /int/ International: >Jason Salesberry, a US Navy veteran, had never set foot on Italian soil before …
entry.content: >Jason Salesberry, a US Navy veteran, had never set foot on Italian soil before moving there in October 2017. >Jason says he had grown increasingly concerned about violent crimes in Chesapeake, Virginia, where the family had been living since 2009. He feels that Naples provides a safer environment for his daughter, now 10. >Today, he owns a house in a hilltop town and is planning the harvest from his two-acre grove of olive trees. Why are so many Americans moving to Europe?
entry.dc:creator: /int/ International

entry.title: /int/ International: On Xitter, I've seen people from poor third-world countries like Mexico and Braz…
entry.content: On Xitter, I've seen people from poor third-world countries like Mexico and Brazil watch *Squid Game* or *Parasite* and genuinely believe that's what South Korea is actually like. They're so clueless that there's really nothing I can say to them.

entry.title: /mu/ Music: What are the best musical performances you've ever witnessed. Not studio tracks …
entry.content: What are the best musical performances you've ever witnessed. Not studio tracks necessarily, but impromptu recordings, radio spots, live sessions, anything. Because it's not always about the song, or the singer, but about both, and the performance, and the circumstance. I'll start. Changes covered by Charles Bradley for the Toronto Star https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zfa Of70M4xs I want a thread of performances that hit you, right in the soul.
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /g/ Technology: What is the best best AI for coding that is free and not local? I want to maximi…
entry.content: What is the best best AI for coding that is free and not local? I want to maximize the amount of free credits bigtech gives me to mod some videogames with thousands of already existing mods. And I don't want local AI because my PC is shit and LLMs that you could run on my machine would probably not be capable of writing code that compiles. So far the best imo is Gemini Flash 3.5 because its better then Sonnet 5 in quality and in service better then 5 daily prompts that ChatGPT gives you.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

She opens a hotel in hopes that patients will be "checking out" into Heaven. Hazbin Hotel Pilot: https://www.

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: The internet is in absolutely dire need of a complete overhaul on who is and isn…
entry.content: The internet is in absolutely dire need of a complete overhaul on who is and isn't allowed to use it. I think that it should be treated in the same fashion as the laws required to operate a motorized vehicle. In order to do that, the law demands that a driver have a license, their vehicle be registered, and have proof of car insurance. Therefore, the ideal internet must have all of its users be licensed (I.E. they must be at least 18 years old, show that they know how to properly use a fucking c
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Alexa Plus
entry.content: Friendly reminder to tell your Alexa "Alexa, disable Alexa Plus" Amazon has quietly "upgraded" everyone to a trial Alexa+ again. You should have noticed it in at least 3 different areas. 1. Different voice without ability to choose original. 2. Refuses to follow instructions, like will gaslight you that you do not have AC which you can turn on/off. 3. Lost ability to simply ping back, like when you ask to turn off lights, because its a chatbot, instead of giving you a gentle ping that task is co
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Having decided that rock-and-roll was fun, a good enough idea within reason, he …
entry.content: Having decided that rock-and-roll was fun, a good enough idea within reason, he then determined that fun consisted not so much of energy and sex and high spirits as it did aimless whimsy and here he finally achieves disaster with that idea. The worst album yet made by a rock-and-roller of the first rank, unless David Crosby counts. D+
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /news/ News: Seething leftist gets caught beating child, gets put on ICE.
entry.content: https://www.rawstory.com/trump-ice- 2677228675/ A Canadian woman got into a physical altercation with a young Trump supporter on the Jersey Shore — and the incident ended with her under arrest by Immigration and Customs Enforcement. According to the New York Post, police have confirmed 33-year-old Kaitlyn Tracey "was taken into custody after she filmed herself confronting two girls wearing 'patriotic colored' sweatpants over Fourth of July weekend at Point Pleasant, one of whom she struck across

entry.title: /g/ Technology: why do people keep shilling macs?
entry.content: why would someone spend tons of money for a laptop with soldered ram and ssd, with 2 usb c ports and a locked os where lots of sw is missing or costs and even some browser extensions cost? Unless you are a gen z or foid tech illiterate who buys it for social status, why would one lock themselves in such a consumerist walled garden?
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: please help (biocomputing)
entry.content: found a big green fly in my kitchen. very healthy, so i put it in my freezer for hibernation purposes. but i need advice. got the pinout for the usb interface, trying to install windows 11 on this fly. its a big fly, so it should be 64-bit, at least 300k neurons but idk. anyway i cant seem to get the fly working. it buzzes but i do not hear copilot activating. i have tried everything... isopropyl, wd-40, a UV flashlight. no dice! i really need this to work. it is a large fly, it may have potenti
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Senior defense officials looking at Cuba military options
entry.content: https://www.cbsnews.com/news/cuba-m ilitary-options-pentagon/ Washington — As the U.S.-Iran war restarts following the collapse of the weeks-long ceasefire, senior Pentagon officials are also quietly eyeing another flashpoint much closer to home: Cuba. Military planners have in recent weeks examined a range of options for possible action against the island, including an Army-led air assault involving thousands of U.S. soldiers to be carried out by the 101st Airborne Division, the only unit train
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /int/ International: In Brazil it's normal to have sex without shame
entry.content: In Brazil there's a term that's untranslatable to English called "Prazsexo" it looks like sex but that's not Portuguese for sex that would be sexy. Prazsexo is a word meaning "Joyful sex" or "Sex of joy" it's like when you have sex and don't worry about social repercussions. It's not love sex even it's more like "I'm young, there's beaches, there's women, there's food, there's Bossa nova playing, I have one life... I want sex with a beautiful woman tonight" And as a white man living in a protest
entry.dc:creator: /int/ International

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Chinese CXMT to Match Micron's DRAM Manufacturing Capacity This Year
entry.content: Chinese memory giant CXMT has been expanding its capacity so rapidly that the company is now able to compete with one of the largest memory manufacturers in the world—Micron. According to analysis from Citrini Research and their proprietary data, CXMT is projected to produce about 350,000 DRAM wafers per month by the end of 2026, assuming all current projects are completed on schedule. This is an impressive figure, especially considering that Micron, one of the "big three" DRAM manufacturers, is
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Reverse image searching is dead. Google images doesn't work because of its shitt…
entry.content: Reverse image searching is dead. Google images doesn't work because of its shitty lens feature, and won't show results for pages that I know for an absolute fact have the image I'm looking for. Yandex has gone to shit and isn't good for anything other than generic photos of people. SauceNAO seemingly stopped working entirely and any artwork I put in there that I know is on X or Pixiv just doesn't show up at all no matter how old or safe for work it is. IQDB is just full of constant errors. TinEy
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /int/ International: I feel like automatic translators and TTS do a terrible job with my language. Ev…

entry.title: /g/ Technology: This shit fucking sucks. The UI is janky as hell, you need a million plugins jus…
entry.content: This shit fucking sucks. The UI is janky as hell, you need a million plugins just to get it to do all the things Plex can do out of the box, and its scanning engine is excruciatingly slow. It took over 60 hours to finish scanning my library of 6000 files. Why do freetards keep shilling it when Plex is also completely free (even for remote streaming if you use a 3rd party player to do it).
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: I fell for the OLED meme.
entry.content: Vertical banding is awful. The internet downplays this issue. The common sentiment is that this is only visible on uniformly grey backgrounds. I find it noticable almost everywhere, even in bright vibrant movies. I guess it's back to IPS monitors until we get QDEL.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >Wasm Containers Docker /b/ros how fucked are we?

entry.title: /g/ Technology: AI is already OP
entry.content: I'm not an employer but a student. I do a Msc degree in a combination of mathematics and physics. It is very surprising to me that AI is able to solve the exercises in the books meant for students at my level. Perhaps, it's only "remembering" a solution that it found on the internet. But even if, it is very effectively explaining it to me. Better than professors do at my university. It is saving up time that I would be researching. It is not a gimmick, at all. It has increased my productivity. I
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology
